pitch.s1_title: Nepal runs on tribal tacit knowledge.
pitch.s1_sub: And we want to change that.
pitch.s2_kicker: What happened to me last month
pitch.s2_body: I tried to register a company and get a PAN. Tripureshwor refused my submission and asked me to refill the form. Kalimati told me Kirtipur isn't their jurisdiction. Kalanki finally accepted me — on the fourth form, in the third office, three weeks in.
pitch.s2_punch: None of this jurisdiction information was on any website.
pitch.s3_quote: They're not charging you for a service. They're charging you for information they've carefully made hard to find.
pitch.s3_body: The websites look modern. The phones don't get answered. The unwritten rules are the entire economy. Middlemen aren't a bug — they're what fills the gap between published rules and real ones.
pitch.s4_h1: What if every government office had one helpdesk?
pitch.s4_sub: Answers in Nepali, Roman-Nepali, or English. Every claim cites its source. When it doesn't know, it says so honestly.
pitch.s5_sub: Open in another tab and ask a question in Nepali. Watch the citations appear. Then ask something we don't know.
pitch.s5_hint: (when it doesn't know, the answer is "call Hello Sarkar 1111" — never made-up)
pitch.s6_card1_body: A crawler keeps a fresh copy of every federal, provincial, and palika site. Old fonts (Preeti / Kantipur) auto-converted to Unicode. PDFs read out.
pitch.s6_card2_body: What's not on the web — counter routes, busy times, paperwork pitfalls — recorded with consent, transcribed, reviewed before publication.
pitch.s6_card3_body: Fine-tuned for Nepal-government Q&A. Free to use. Runs on a Raspberry Pi or any office computer — no Google or OpenAI account needed. Your data stays in Nepal.
pitch.s6b_kicker: Under the hood — for the curious
pitch.s6b_h1: Open-source pipeline. Cited answers. No big-tech account.
pitch.s6b_step1_body: Citizen asks in Nepali, Roman-Nepali, or English
pitch.s6b_step2_body: English / Roman-NE words auto-expand to Devanagari before searching
pitch.s6b_step3_body: Top matches from 270,509 indexed chunks · plus citizen-interview claims
pitch.s6b_step4_body: Gemma 4 E2B (Google open model) + 474 MB LoRA fine-tune for grounding & refusal
pitch.s6b_step5_body: Every claim carries [source URL]. No match → "call Hello Sarkar 1111"
pitch.s6b_stack_crawler: Rust crawler · 899 .gov.np sources · 22 modules / 24 test suites · Preeti → Unicode · PDF + HTML
pitch.s6b_stack_retrieval: SQLite FTS5 · 270k chunks · bilingual anchor map (English/Roman ↔ Devanagari)
pitch.s6b_stack_composer: Gemma 4 E2B-IT (open weights) · LoRA adapter trained on grounded answers + refusal + anti-template slices
pitch.s6b_stack_hardware: Inference: Mac Studio M2 Ultra (MPS) · model + adapter < 6 GB → runs on a Raspberry Pi
pitch.s6b_stack_deploy: Hetzner ARM (€4/mo) + Traefik + Let's Encrypt · Tailscale → office Mac
pitch.s6b_callout: Open weights · Local inference · Your data stays in Nepal
pitch.s7_h1: Your website is already in our corpus.
pitch.s7_body: Jirimun.gov.np is one of the 899 sources we crawl. The model can already cite your service catalog, ward boundaries, contact pages.
pitch.s7_gap_kicker: What's missing — and only you can give us:
pitch.s7_gap_2: Best and worst times of day
pitch.s7_gap_4: How to handle Kirtipur-style jurisdiction edge cases
pitch.s8_h1: 30 minutes. One senior staff member. 8 questions.
pitch.s8_sub: We come to your office. We record audio. You see the result before anything publishes.
pitch.s9_sub: धन्यवाद · Thank you for your time.

admin.title: Admin
admin.subtitle: Review interview submissions
admin.login_body: Enter the admin credentials shared with you to review submissions.
admin.approve_confirm_body: Vertex AI will transcribe each audio clip. Transcripts will be added to the tacit corpus and the retriever will reload. This is irreversible.
admin.reject_confirm_body: The submission will be moved to the rejected archive. The audio and photos will be kept on disk for forensics.

interview.title: Tacit Collection
interview.subtitle: Share what you know about a Nepal-government office
interview.intro_fallback: Eight short questions. Speak in whichever language is comfortable — Nepali, Roman-Nepali, English, or mixed. Your audio is the source of truth.
interview.your_office: Office (where you work / what you describe)
interview.photos_hint: Tap to add photos · max 5 · 5 MB each
interview.submit_validation_name: Please fill in your name and office.
interview.submit_validation_audio: Record at least one answer before submitting.
interview.submit_success_body: Your contribution has been received. Our team will review and add the useful parts to the helpdesk.

chat.title: Ask the helpdesk
chat.subtitle: Bilingual answers grounded in .gov.np + tacit knowledge
chat.placeholder: Ask in Nepali, Roman-Nepali, or English…
chat.empty_body: Ask anything about Nepal-government services. Answers cite their sources, or refuse honestly when none match.
chat.example_1: How do I replace a lost citizenship certificate?
chat.example_2: What time does Jiri Municipality open?

landing.title: A helpdesk that actually answers Nepal-government questions
landing.tagline: Ask in Nepali, Roman-Nepali, or English. Answers cite real .gov.np sources and tacit knowledge from people who work the counter — not hallucinated guesses.
landing.problem_body_1: Nepal's government services are scattered across hundreds of .gov.np websites — many in legacy fonts (Preeti, Kantipur), behind PDFs no search engine indexes, with procedural details that change ward-by-ward.
landing.problem_body_2: Hello Sarkar 1111 helps over the phone, but is unreachable from a queue at 9 AM. Search engines return form templates, not "which counter do I go to first." The practical knowledge — the thing every experienced agent knows in their head — rarely makes it onto a webpage.
landing.feature_corpus_body: A custom Rust crawler keeps a living corpus of federal, provincial, and palika sites — Devanagari + Preeti font conversion, PDF text extraction, deduplication, freshness scoring.
landing.feature_tacit_body: Citizens, ward officers, and consultants record what they know about the offices they work — counter routes, timing, paperwork pitfalls. Stored as immutable audio, curated by an admin before joining the corpus.
landing.feature_model_body: Built on Google's Gemma 4 E2B, fine-tuned with a refusal-when-no-source discipline and bilingual citation grammar. Composer over a hybrid retrieval pipeline — not a free-form chatbot.
landing.feature_refuse_body: Every factual claim carries the source URL it came from. If no source meaningfully addresses your question, the system says so — and tells you to call Hello Sarkar 1111.
landing.how_citizen_body: Question → retrieval over both corpora → composed answer with citations. Public, no auth required.
landing.how_interview_body: Anyone records short answers per question. Audio is the source of truth. Admin reviews, transcribes via Vertex, then promotes to corpus.
landing.how_runtime_body: The model lives on a Mac Studio (M2 Ultra) over Tailscale. A Hetzner reverse-proxy serves the public domain.
landing.stack_crawler_value: Rust + SQLite + recipe-driven crawl over 899 sources
landing.stack_retrieval_value: SQLite FTS5 + bilingual anchor expansion (English/Roman-NE → Devanagari)
landing.stack_composer_value: Gemma 4 E2B-IT + LoRA adapter trained on grounded-answer / refusal / anti-template slices
landing.stack_server_value: FastAPI on Mac Studio M2 Ultra · transformers + MPS
landing.stack_asr_value: Vertex AI (Gemini 2.5) for transcription on admin approve
landing.stack_hosting_value: Traefik + Let's Encrypt on Hetzner Cloud → tailnet → k2
landing.try_chat_body: Open the chat, type in any of three languages. You'll get an answer with cited sources or an honest refusal.
landing.try_interview_body: Work at a gov office? Visited one recently? Record a few minutes of practical advice — what counter, what hours, what to bring.
landing.footer_tagline: Real answers about your government, in your language.
landing.footer_help: Still need a human? Call Hello Sarkar 1111.
